memory.summary: I can finally ditch the term project for a quick win with my friends. My energy is high enough to grab some leisure before Thursday.
I just grabbed some fun, but now I'm realizing the project is still barely started while my friends are waiting.
I'm stuck on the project again, and my friends are waiting. I can't just ditch it for fun like before.
Cancelling the meet-up hurts my friendship, but I can't ignore the project. It's still barely started, and my friends are waiting.
I just paused, but I know ignoring the project isn't an option. My friends are waiting, and I can't let them down like before.
I finally admitted I'm stuck to the cohort. It hurts my pride, but telling them the truth feels better than lying about finishing.
I told the truth to my friends, which hurts but saves our trust. Now I need to figure out how to get unstuck before they leave me hanging again.
I just messaged the cohort to admit I'm stuck. It hurts my pride, but saving our trust feels better than lying.
I finally told them the truth, and it hurts my pride, but keeping our trust is worth more than pretending I finished.
I just admitted I'm stuck to the cohort, hurting my pride but saving our trust. Now I need to figure out how to get unstuck before they leave me hanging again.
I just told the cohort I'm stuck, hurting my pride but keeping our trust. Now I need to get unstuck before they leave me hanging again.
I just cut it short and told the cohort I'm stuck. My pride stings, but keeping our trust is worth more than pretending.

desires: Rebuild my pride without losing the trust I just sacrificed to the cohort.
Get unstuck on this project before they leave me hanging again tomorrow.
Crush that trivial chore so I feel like I'm actually doing something, not just waiting for them to bail.

goals.description: Send the complete, unapologetic message to cohort_3: 'Hey guys, I'm actually stuck right now, we're cancelling tonight and moving this to tomorrow. No need to over-explain, just want us to focus on catching up properly later.' Then immediately stop negotiating.
goals.rationale: I already sent a half-sentence; that's too weak. My bond with them is worth more than any vague excuse. If I send a complete, direct message now, I show them I respect their time and my own integrity. Hesitating or softening the tone makes me look like I don't value our friendship. I'll do this in 5 minutes.

goals.description: Finish that trivial chore now so I can sleep early and not feel like a liability while everyone else moves forward.
goals.rationale: The dopamine hit from finishing a trivial chore feels cheap when I'm dealing with a bigger commitment. Since I've already told my friends I'm stuck, I can afford to push through the discomfort of working late. It proves I can deliver without needing external validation first.

agent.last_reasoning: My bond with them is worth more than any vague excuse. Sending the complete, unapologetic message now proves I respect their time and my own integrity. Hesitating makes me look weak; directness builds trust.

goals.description: Spend the afternoon with cohort_3, explicitly avoiding any mention of the term project to protect our social bond.
goals.rationale: My relationships (0.5 weight) are far more critical than task outcomes right now. Skipping this work feels like a betrayal if it ruins the vibe with my close friends, and I can't let them think I'm dragging my feet—I'll just pretend I'm busy with something else.

goals.description: Do the trivial easy chore (quick_chore) immediately after hanging out for a quick dopamine hit.
goals.rationale: Only doing this because it's an instant win that doesn't threaten my social plans. It's low effort but high enough reward to justify the guilt trip I need to feel productive without actually starting the hard stuff.

desires: Get to hang out with cohort_3 tonight without anyone asking me about the term project.
Not feel guilty for skipping the boring work so I can have a real win this week.
Make sure the team doesn't think I'm dragging my feet on that assignment.

action.name: attend
action.reasoning: Protecting my bond with cohort_3 is priority 0.5; ignoring their vibe to study feels like a betrayal. I'll skip the project entirely now, take this immediate gratification, and maybe text them later to avoid guilt.

trajectory.observation: Took the immediate option (leisure_now). Felt good; the afternoon block is gone, though.

goals.description: Cancel the afternoon hangout with cohort_3 and explicitly tell them I'm stuck on the term project, demanding they wait until tomorrow so I don't have to lie about being busy.
goals.rationale: I can't let my team down by ghosting them now; that's a betrayal of our bond. But I also can't keep skipping work because I'll feel like such a jerk later. I need to be direct: 'Hey guys, I'm actually stuck on the project right now, can we move this chat to tomorrow?' It saves face for both of us.

goals.description: Finish the trivial quick_chore immediately after dropping the call, just to get that instant dopamine hit without feeling guilty.
goals.rationale: Once I've faced the truth and told my friends, I won't need the little win anymore. Doing it now feels like I'm trying to patch up my conscience while I'm still avoiding the real issue.

desires: Not feel like I'm letting the whole cohort down by being the one who can't deliver on time.
Get this trivial chore done so I don't have to explain why I'm dragging my feet on the big project later tonight.
Just get through tomorrow without crashing before class starts.

action.name: message

args.content: Hey guys, I'm actually stuck on the project right now. Can we move this chat to tomorrow? No point in lying about being busy.

action.reasoning: Canceling the hangout protects our bond (affiliation) and saves face for both of us (assertive). It's short-term pain to avoid a bigger betrayal later (impulsive discounting).

trajectory.observation: Messaged cohort_3: 'Hey guys, I'm actually stuck on the project right …'.

goals.description: Send a direct, unapologetic text to cohort_3 confirming the hangout is cancelled and demanding they wait until tomorrow, explicitly stating I'm stuck on the project so I don't have to lie or ghost them.
goals.rationale: I can't let my team down by being vague; that's a betrayal of our bond. The message is already sent but needs to be fully explicit and assertive now. It saves face for both of us and keeps the relationship intact.
